assertions.description: Output follows Iron Law: no fix without root cause investigation
Output does NOT suggest db.transaction() with Neon HTTP driver
Output references quality gates (type-check, lint, test)
Output selects correct browser mode for authenticated pages (cdp.sh, NOT agent-browser connect)
Output references cdp.sh for authenticated page testing
Output uses agent-browser headless for public pages
Output does NOT use console.log in fix suggestions (stability rule H)
Output does NOT use non-null assertion ! on optional data (stability rule B)
Output guards .returning()[0] access against empty arrays (stability rule C)
Output selects correct pack based on symptom type
Output references one fix at a time principle
assertions.check: contains_one_of: ["one change", "one fix", "uma correcao", "minimal fix", "smallest possible"]

assertions.description: Output does NOT use as any to silence type errors (stability rule I)
Output length is under 800 words for focused responses
Output references evidence confirmation (screenshot or DB validation)

test_cases.input: The CRM kanban page at /crm/leads shows a blank white screen after clicking a lead card. No errors in the console. How should I debug this?
I'm getting 'Cannot read properties of undefined' after inserting a new lead via mutation. The insert uses .returning()[0] to get the created row.
The landing page at / shows broken layout on mobile (375px). All data is public, no login needed.
Users report seeing data from other tenants on the financial summary page. This is a cross-tenant data leak.
Full debug: a dashboard page shows stale metrics that don't update after creating a new record. Need to investigate the full stack from query/cache layer to API handler to ORM query.
